claims.evidence_text: Minimum falling number for milling grades (300 seconds) is indicated by the solid line.
claims.claim_summary: Minimum falling number for wheat milling grades is 300 seconds.

claims.evidence_text: detrimental levels of PHS are occurring during the maturation drying period and well before harvest maturity (12-14% moisture content) has been reached
claims.claim_summary: Wheat harvest maturity corresponds to about 12-14% grain moisture content.

claims.evidence_text: These crops are exhibiting pre-harvest sprouting ... from soon after mass maturity (maximum grain dry weight, approximately 40 % moisture content)
claims.claim_summary: Wheat reaches mass/physiological maturity (maximum grain dry weight) at approximately 40% grain moisture content.

claims.evidence_text: earlier maturing crops (resulting from early sowing dates and quick developing varieties) show increased pre-harvest sprouting incidence ... occurring during the maturation drying period and well before harvest maturity
claims.claim_summary: Wheat pre-harvest sprouting risk increases with cool/wet maturation and earlier maturation; detrimental falling-number decline begins soon after mass maturity (~40% moisture), before harvest maturity. Managed via cultivar PHS resistance and sowing date.

claims.evidence_text: small plots of 8 m length by 6 rows (25.7 cm row spacing on 1.85 m plot centres)
claims.claim_summary: Wheat trial plots used 25.7 cm row spacing.
